title: Runnable examples are tested contracts
description: An example that is executed in CI is a contract that catches drift, while an unverified snippet rots silently into a wrong instruction.
body: Use when deciding how much to trust or invest in a doc example. An example wired into CI (doctest, sample build, snippet runner) fails the build when the API changes, so it stays correct; a copy-pasted snippet that nothing executes drifts the moment the code moves. Do not present unverified snippets as authoritative without marking them illustrative. Actions: extract examples into runnable, CI-checked sources where feasible. Validation signals include the example suite passing in CI and a failure when the API is intentionally changed. Risk boundary: an out-of-date example is worse than none because readers trust and run it; mark anything unrunnable as illustrative.
